en: Thank you very much. Thank you.
I haven't actually been here myself before, but I hear some frightfully good reports about it all.
They're doing some rather interesting things, you know.
I wanted anyway, my dear, to bring you to a place that had a certain sort of ambience.
You mean atmosphere. Atmosphere, dear, yes. Ambience.
Yes. Karen, we've been going out together now for three years, and I was wondering...
Yes? ...if this would make you look taller.
And your name? I am Ali Nadeem, from Lahore.
I'm working at the moment, not anywhere at all.
You're unemployed? Yes, please. Only one day a week I'm working.
What do you do then? I'm going to the unemployment exchange, for to be collecting my money.
Oh, blimey!
I get more money for not being working than when I'm working.
Yes, but before you discovered this secret of eternal wealth, what did you do?
Blimey, I worked. At the Taj Mahal. In Delhi?
Oh, no. Patni. Taj Mahal Kandiri restaurant.
Jolly good chapatti and papadum.
When I bang my clipboard on the dash like so...
I'd like you to imagine someone stepped in front of the car,
and execute an emergency stop, retaining full control of the vehicle.
OK. Would you please pull away when it is safe to do so?
Thank you. Who's next? Me, please.
Tea or coffee? Hot water. Hot water?
Yes, please. I bring my own tea bag. You can't do that.
But I'm already doing it. I'm starting Economy Ride.
But that tea bag's already been used. Most definitely.
I'm making three cups of tea out of one tea baggie.
Well, I'm sorry, you can't make your own tea.
Oh, blimey. Then I'm not having any at all. I have three children, Jeremy, Alison and Jerry.
My wife and I have been married for eight years.
I have two children, Derek and Doreen.
My wife and I have been married for six years. I have eight children.
Peter, Rita, Annika, Sandra, Dawn, Clint, Rock and Elvis.
My wife and I are not married.
My wife is on several committees, runs the local opera group and is secretary of the Bridge Club.
My wife is a member of the Mother's Union, enjoys organising coffee mornings and is a driver for Meals on Wheels.
My wife is pregnant again. Are you a doctor?
Careful. Not a doctor. I'm a brain surgeon.
Big difference. Big difference. What do you guys do? I'm an accountant.
Oh, that's good. Still, it's not exactly brain surgery, is it?
Lionel, here's your drink. Lionel's a brain surgeon, you know.
Yeah, he mentioned it. Oh, Geoff, I keep you late at the Space Centre.
As always. Have you met Lionel? No. Hello, Lionel.
So, Geoff, how do you earn a crust? Well, I'm a scientist. I work mainly with rockets.
It's pretty tough work. What do you do?
Well, I don't mean to boast, but I'm a brain surgeon.
Brain surgery?
Not exactly rocket science, is it?
Switch it off. Switch it off!
Hey! Switch it off!
Well, we've had your test results back, and you'll be pleased to hear that it's benign.
Oh, thank goodness. That is such a relief. Yeah, it's good news, isn't it, Mr Ignant?
Yeah, it's good news. Yeah, it's good news. Yeah, it's good news. I'm not Mr Ignant. I'm Mr Ein. Mr Ben Ein.
Well, who's this Mal Ignant? Well, we've had your test results back,
and you'll be pleased to hear that it's benign. Oh, thank goodness. That is such a relief.
Please be seated.
Welcome, friends and family, and of course, Archibald and Poppy.
Let us pray you never marry. Let us pray.
You never marry without love. You never love without God.
God is almighty. God is everywhere.
God is touching you all in your private places.
God is touching you all. In your private places, in your darkest times, God is there shining a light,
and today he's shining a light for these two assholes.
And today he's shining a light for these two.
Our souls are eternally in his debt.
Excuse me. Is anyone sitting there? No. Is anyone sitting there?
No. Is anyone sitting there? No.
You've got no friends, mate.
Are you all right there, Tim? You seem to be struggling a bit. Yeah, I am a bit, yeah. It's incredibly uncomfortable.
I know I'm tall, but is it really supposed to be as painful as this?
Well, let's find out what's wrong, shall we?
Your right foot should be about here, OK? Yeah, that's right.
And your left foot should be about here. No.
It's here.
Oh, you haven't been operated on by Dr Theobald, have you?
Yeah, I have, yeah. What do you mean, oh?
When he operated on me, he left a scalpel inside me.
Left a surgical mask inside me. Left a syringe in me.
Great.
